organism: # SAA1 (Homo sapiens): UniProt left as NA. Candidate accessions are P0DJI8 and P02763
#   (both annotated as SAA1_HUMAN in different UniProt releases); verify current
# IL17C (Homo sapiens): Candidate UniProt Q9P0M4 (IL17C_HUMAN) — not verified against
#   live UniProt; leave as NA until confirmed.
# H2BC4 (Homo sapiens): H2B variants have complex, frequently revised nomenclature.
#   Candidate accession P62807 (H2BC4_HUMAN / H2B type 1-J), but the user-specified
#   "H2B variants" may refer to multiple paralogs (H2BC4, H2BC5, H2BC11, etc.).
#   Expand to full variant list and verify accessions before PPI queries.
# SirTM (Parvimonas micra): Protein class (bacterial sirtuin / SirTM) is documented in
#   anaerobic bacteria but a P. micra-specific UniProt entry has not been verified.
#   Search UniProt with taxon "Parvimonas micra" + keyword "sirtuin" to find the
#   correct accession. Source PMID also left as NA — candidate literature includes
#   Rack et al. on bacterial sirtuins, but PMID not confirmed.
# Bacterial LPS/adhesin placeholders (P. stomatis, G. morbillorum, D. pneumosintes):
#   All three entries are scaffolds only. Specific gene products (e.g. FadA-like
#   adhesins, Lpx-family LPS enzymes) must be identified via genome annotation
#   (NCBI RefSeq) and literature before UniProt IDs or PMIDs can be filled in.
# All source_pmid fields set to NA: a systematic PubMed search linking each host
#   protein to eoCRC/CRC and to the four bacterial species is required. Starting
#   points: Flemer et al. 2018 (P. micra CRC association), Drewes et al. 2022
